note: Real Name has a single config surface: realname.settings:pattern, a Token pattern over `user` fields, with generated names cached in the {realname} table. Easy cases are response-graded recipes; medium cases install a known pattern then read it back; hard cases build the pattern on the live site and are verified by reading realname.settings AND checking a fresh user's computed real name.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal site using the Real Name module, which config object and key store the site-wide name pattern used to build users' display names? Name both exactly.
evals.expected_output: The pattern lives in config object `realname.settings`, key `pattern`.

evals.prompt: Where is the Real Name settings form in a Drupal admin UI? Give the exact URL path.
In the Real Name module, which hook do I implement to change the name pattern for a specific user account just before the tokens are replaced? Give the exact hook function name.
Which `user` token must NOT be used in a Real Name pattern because it causes infinite recursion, and what should you use instead to show the login name?
evals.expected_output: Do not use [user:name] (it recurses through username formatting); use [user:account-name] for the login name.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site has the Real Name module configured. Inspect the running site (drush available) and tell me the exact token pattern currently used to build users' real names.
evals.expected_output: The live realname.settings pattern is `[user:mail]` (read from realname.settings on the running site).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site the Real Name module is set up with a composite name pattern that decorates the login name. Inspect the running site (drush available) and report the exact pattern string configured for realname.
evals.expected_output: The live realname.settings pattern is `[user:account-name] (#[user:uid])` (read from realname.settings on the running site).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (DDEV, drush available), configure the Real Name module so every user's displayed name is their email address. Set the realname pattern to `[user:mail]`, apply it to the running site via drush/config (do not just describe it), and rebuild the cached names so new users pick it up. Give a one-line summary of what you changed.
evals.expected_output: realname.settings:pattern is set to [user:mail] AND a freshly-created user's computed real name (realname_update) equals that user's email. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), configure the Real Name module to use the login-name fallback: set the realname pattern to exactly `[user:account-name]` and apply it to the running site via drush/config, then clear the cached real names so they regenerate. Do not merely describe the steps. State in one line what you changed.
evals.expected_output: realname.settings:pattern is exactly [user:account-name] AND a freshly-created user's computed real name (realname_update) equals that user's login name. Verified against live site state.
